articles.title: LeBron James Becomes First Player To Reach Significant Playoff Milestone
articles.original_title: LeBron James Becomes First Player To Reach Significant Playoff Milestone
articles.summary: Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James became the first player in NBA history to make 3,000 career playoff field goals. James accomplished the feat with his second basket of Game 4 against the Houston Rockets. James has a sizable lead on Kareem Abdul-Jabbar (2,356) and Michael Jordan (2,188) for most-made playoff field goals and will […]

articles.title: The end of the LeBron/AD era was really a missed opportunity
articles.original_title: The end of the LeBron/AD era was really a missed opportunity
articles.summary: Being up 3-1 in the first round with Father Time Lebron + role players has me feeling some type of way about the previous Lakers era.  Lakers Nation was begging for years for Rob to get a 2-way POA defender like Marcus Smart. Instead we were blursed with the cadavers of Kendrick Nunn and Gabe Vincent + streaky D-Lo (won't even talk about the expensive PG we traded all our good role players for) that constantly relied on AD to bail them out on defense which undoubtedly contributed to his multiple

articles.title: Divac on passing on Luka: “Luka is a point guard. Luka is a coach on the floor. And I felt that if I get Luka, I would have to trade De’Aaron Fox, and I already got a relationship with Fox, and I see he’s a perfect fit for us, a small market team, and Luka was a big market player”
articles.original_title: Divac on passing on Luka: “Luka is a point guard. Luka is a coach on the floor. And I felt that if I get Luka, I would have to trade De’Aaron Fox, and I already got a relationship with Fox, and I see he’s a perfect fit for us, a small market team, and Luka was a big market player”

articles.original_title: Pushed to brink, 60-win Pistons vow 'to be better'
articles.summary: With another lackluster performance that included 20 turnovers, Detroit, the No.  1 seed in the Eastern Conference, lost to the Orlando Magic in Game 4 on Monday night, a result that shoved star Cade Cunningham and the Pistons to the brink of elimination.

articles.title: Jokic's triple-double powers Nuggets to a Game 6
articles.original_title: Jokic's triple-double powers Nuggets to a Game 6
articles.summary: Nikola Jokic snapped out of his prolonged funk with a triple-double, Spencer Jones provided a key spark, and the Nuggets staved off elimination with a chippy 125-113 win over the injury-riddled Timberwolves in Game 5 of their playoff series Monday night.

articles.original_title: Jones 1st to plead guilty in NBA gambling case

articles.title: Lakers Add Deandre Ayton Replacement in First Round of 2026 NBA Mock Draft
articles.original_title: Lakers Add Deandre Ayton Replacement in First Round of 2026 NBA Mock Draft - heavy.com
articles.summary: A recent mock draft from heavy.com shows the Los Angeles Lakers selecting a player to replace former center Deandre Ayton in the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft. The pick reflects the franchise’s continued focus on bolstering its front‑court depth as it plans for the post‑LeBron era.
key_points: Mock draft projects the Lakers selecting a new big man to fill the void left by Ayton.
The choice underscores the team's strategy to rebuild around younger talent.
Analysts note the pick could impact the Lakers' salary‑cap flexibility in upcoming seasons.

articles.title: LeBron James Becomes First Player To Reach Significant Playoff Milestone
articles.original_title: LeBron James Becomes First Player To Reach Significant Playoff Milestone - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: Lakers Nation reports that LeBron James has become the first player in NBA history to achieve a new playoff benchmark, surpassing previous records for combined playoff points, rebounds, and assists. The milestone highlights James’ longevity and sustained elite performance in postseason play.
key_points: James set a new all‑time playoff record for cumulative statistical categories.
The achievement came during the Lakers’ recent playoff run.
It adds to James’ legacy as the most durable and productive postseason performer.

articles.title: Luka Dončić’s Injury Status Ahead of Game 5 vs. Rockets
articles.original_title: Is Luka Dončić Playing in Game 5? Latest Update on Lakers Star’s Injury Status vs. Rockets - PFSN
articles.summary: PFSN provides an update on Luka Dončić’s health ahead of the Lakers’ Game 5 matchup against the Houston Rockets. The report indicates that the star guard remains questionable, with the team monitoring his ankle swelling and limiting his practice minutes.
key_points: Dončić is listed as questionable for Game 5 due to an ankle issue.
Coaches are limiting his workload in practice to aid recovery.
The Lakers’ game plan may shift if he is unable to play.

articles.title: Lakers Insider Estimates Luka Dončić’s Return Timeline
articles.original_title: Lakers insider estimates Luka Doncic's return timeline - LeBron Wire
articles.summary: LeBron Wire cites a Lakers insider who projects Luka Dončić’s return to the lineup within 7‑10 days, pending clearance from the team’s medical staff. The timeline suggests the star could be back for the second round of the playoffs if his rehab progresses as expected.
key_points: Insider predicts a 7‑10 day recovery window for Dončić.
Medical staff will conduct final clearance before he returns.
His return could bolster the Lakers’ chances in the upcoming playoff series.

articles.title: Why Has Scoring Taken a Nosedive in the NBA Playoffs?
articles.original_title: Why Has Scoring Taken a Nosedive in the NBA Playoffs? - The Ringer
articles.summary: The Ringer examines the recent decline in scoring averages during the NBA postseason, attributing the trend to tighter defenses, slower pace of play, and increased emphasis on three‑point efficiency. The article references several playoff series, including the Lakers’, to illustrate the shift.
key_points: Defensive schemes have become more sophisticated, limiting high‑scoring games.
Teams are adopting slower tempos and prioritizing efficient shot selection.
The trend impacts star players, forcing them to adapt their offensive approaches.
